items.headline: Major Elevator Upgrade Project at Ridder Park Drive
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator modernization project is underway at 1290 Ridder Park Dr, likely affecting a significant commercial or mixed-use building. This substantial infrastructure investment suggests major improvements to a key commercial property in San José.

items.headline: Cupertino sued over Mary Avenue safety concerns
items.summary: The Garden Gate Coalition filed a lawsuit against the City of Cupertino regarding safety issues on Mary Avenue. This ongoing litigation could affect traffic and pedestrian safety decisions in that area.

items.headline: Commercial/Industrial Development on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A new $696,570 commercial or industrial project is permitted at 1300 W San Carlos St. This represents actual new commercial development rather than renovation or conversion work.

items.headline: Natural Gas Rates Set to Change in Fiscal 2027
items.summary: City Council approved changes to residential and commercial gas rate schedules for the upcoming fiscal year. Utility rates affect household costs, so residents should check whether their gas bills will increase.

items.headline: City launches business incentive programs for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino approved multiple economic development initiatives including a business license amnesty program, outreach efforts to shopping centers, and incentives for retail and startups. These programs aim to support struggling local businesses and encourage new economic activity.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up designated as annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event and authorized city funds and donations to support it. This community cleanup program will receive ongoing municipal support.

items.headline: Community development grants available for housing programs
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications on March 25. These grants support affordable housing and community services that directly benefit low-income Sunnyvale residents.

items.headline: New Retail Rules Approved to Boost Downtown Shopping Districts
items.summary: Council approved zoning and building code changes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. These changes aim to make it easier to open and maintain retail businesses in Palo Alto's commercial areas.
